arguments.task: Open and read the official Meta Marketing APIs documentation and related first-party pages needed to determine: 1) one-line description of Meta Ads / Marketing API, 2) every documented auth method for developers, 3) whether access is self-serve free, trial, paid, admin approval, or partner/contact-sales gated, 4) API surface type and breadth, 5) whether an MCP server exists from Meta or first-party docs, 6) rate limit notes. Also open canonical first-party pages for Meta app development auth and rate limiting if linked. Return concise notes with the exact URLs actually read and the supporting text for each field. Do not use third-party sources or search snippets.

raw_result.description: Meta’s Marketing API lets developers programmatically manage Facebook ads and related ad assets.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official Marketing API getting-started docs include separate Authentication and Authorization sections, supporting app-based Meta login flows and access-token-based API calls. Evidence gathered showed canonical auth doc routes under the Meta developers site, including /docs/marketing-api/getting-started and /docs/marketing-api/authentication, but the browser session could not reliably render the auth content text end-to-end in this pass.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official docs indicate Marketing API access is controlled through Meta app setup and access levels rather than open anonymous use. Based on the gathered evidence, developers must create a Meta app and obtain the necessary permissions/access before production use.

api_surface.breadth: Broad marketing/ads management surface covering campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, reporting/insights, and other ad account resources within the Marketing API documentation.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party Meta documentation surfaced in this pass indicating an official MCP server for Meta Ads/Marketing API.

raw_result.blocker: Official docs are public, but access depends on Meta app setup, token acquisition, and permission/access-level approval; additionally, some first-party pages were JS-rendered or intermittently blank during verification in this pass.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Meta documents rate limiting for the Marketing API, but the exact rate-limit text was not successfully extracted from a stable first-party page in this pass; expect platform-enforced limits tied to app/ad-account usage.
raw_result.notable_signals: The official Meta Developers site exposes a substantial Marketing API documentation tree and dedicated getting-started/authentication routes, indicating a mature developer surface, but the browser encountered blank/SPA-rendering failures on some canonical pages.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields need confirmation from fully rendered first-party page text: auth_methods could not be exhaustively verified from readable auth content, access_tier depends on exact Meta access-level language, and MCP absence is based on no evidence found rather than an explicit first-party statement.

result.description: Meta’s Marketing API lets developers programmatically manage Facebook ads and related ad assets.
result.auth_notes: The official Marketing API getting-started docs include separate Authentication and Authorization sections, supporting app-based Meta login flows and access-token-based API calls. Evidence gathered showed canonical auth doc routes under the Meta developers site, including /docs/marketing-api/getting-started and /docs/marketing-api/authentication, but the browser session could not reliably render the auth content text end-to-end in this pass.
result.gating_reason: Official docs indicate Marketing API access is controlled through Meta app setup and access levels rather than open anonymous use. Based on the gathered evidence, developers must create a Meta app and obtain the necessary permissions/access before production use.

api_surface.breadth: Broad marketing/ads management surface covering campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, reporting/insights, and other ad account resources within the Marketing API documentation.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party Meta documentation surfaced in this pass indicating an official MCP server for Meta Ads/Marketing API.

result.blocker: Official docs are public, but access depends on Meta app setup, token acquisition, and permission/access-level approval; additionally, some first-party pages were JS-rendered or intermittently blank during verification in this pass.
result.rate_limit_notes: Meta documents rate limiting for the Marketing API, but the exact rate-limit text was not successfully extracted from a stable first-party page in this pass; expect platform-enforced limits tied to app/ad-account usage.
result.notable_signals: The official Meta Developers site exposes a substantial Marketing API documentation tree and dedicated getting-started/authentication routes, indicating a mature developer surface, but the browser encountered blank/SPA-rendering failures on some canonical pages.
result.human_review_reason: Structured fields need confirmation from fully rendered first-party page text: auth_methods could not be exhaustively verified from readable auth content, access_tier depends on exact Meta access-level language, and MCP absence is based on no evidence found rather than an explicit first-party statement.
